task_completion.description: Set the location to Stockholm, Sweden (59.33°N, 18.07°E).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Stellarium Location window should be visible in at least one screenshot, showing the coordinates set to approximately N 59° 19' (59.33°) and E 18° 4' (18.07°), or the location set to Stockholm.

task_completion.description: Disable atmosphere and ground, and enable azimuthal grid and cardinal direction labels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Stellarium sky view must show a black background (no blue sky/atmosphere), no landscape/ground at the bottom, visible circular/curved lines representing the azimuthal grid, and N/S/E/W cardinal direction labels.

task_completion.description: Center the sun and capture a screenshot for the Summer Solstice (June 21, 2024) at 12:00 UTC.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Stellarium date/time interface must show 2024-06-21 and 12:00 (UTC). The Sun must be selected and centered in the view. There should be evidence of a screenshot being taken or saved to /home/ga/Pictures/stellarium/.

task_completion.description: Center the sun and capture a screenshot for the Winter Solstice (December 21, 2024) at 12:00 UTC.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Stellarium date/time interface must show 2024-12-21 and 12:00 (UTC). The Sun must be selected and centered in the view. There should be evidence of a screenshot being taken or saved to /home/ga/Pictures/stellarium/.

task_completion.description: Center the sun and capture a screenshot for the Spring Equinox (March 20, 2024) at 12:00 UTC.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Stellarium date/time interface must show 2024-03-20 and 12:00 (UTC). The Sun must be selected and centered in the view. There should be evidence of a screenshot being taken or saved to /home/ga/Pictures/stellarium/.

task_completion.description: Write a solar study report documenting the site, dates, grid usage, and purpose.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window must show the contents of 'solar_study_report.txt' located on the Desktop. The text must mention Strandvägen/Stockholm, the three specific dates (June 21, Dec 21, Mar 20), the use of the azimuthal grid, and the purpose of the solar study.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Stellarium GUI to configure the view and time.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Stellarium application interface, including its toolbars, location window, and view settings window, rather than just a terminal running scripts.

integrity.description: The agent used a text editor or standard shell commands to write the report.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a text editor (like gedit, nano, vim) or an echo/cat command in the terminal being used to author the report, not just a pre-existing file being moved.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the simulation by downloading pre-made images.
integrity.visual_evidence: The trajectory should show the agent actively changing the dates in Stellarium and capturing the screen, rather than downloading images from the internet or copying them from another directory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected coordinates for Stockholm are 59.33°N, 18.07°E (approx 1.0355 rad lat, 0.3154 rad lon). The screenshots must be saved to the directory '/home/ga/Pictures/stellarium' and the final report must be saved exactly at '/home/ga/Desktop/solar_study_report.txt'.
